data.title: Volvo's US Shift: Li Auto Soars 2.71%
Ben & Jerry's Treats Fans to Free Cone Day in 2025
data.description:
Ben & Jerry's, the beloved ice cream brand, has announced the date for its annual Free Cone Day event in 2025. Mark your calendars for Tuesday, April 8, when participating Scoop Shops worldwide will offer free scoops of their delicious ice cream to customers as a token of gratitude.

The event, which has been a tradition since 1979, will take place from 12 p.m. to 8 p.m. local time. Fans can expect long lines, so it's recommended to bring a foldable chair and dress accordingly. Each person will receive one free cone with one scoop of their favorite ben & Jerry's flavor.

While the event is a great opportunity to enjoy a free treat, it's important to note that the offer is limited to one cone per person. However, Ben & Jerry's is known for its generous portions, so customers can still indulge in their favorite flavors without breaking the bank.

In addition to the Free Cone Day event, Ben & Jerry's has also announced a special promotion for customers who purchase their ice cream from Scoop Shops or select retailers. For a limited time, customers will receive a free pair of Ben & Jerry's socks with their purchase. This offer is a great way to show off your love for the brand while enjoying your favorite ice cream flavors.

Ben & Jerry's is not only known for its delicious ice cream but also for its commitment to social and environmental causes. The Free Cone Day event is just one example of how the company gives back to its customers and communities. By offering free ice cream to customers, Ben & Jerry's is able to spread joy and gratitude while also promoting its brand.

As the date for the Free Cone Day event approaches, customers can expect to see promotions and advertisements from Ben & Jerry's. The company is known for its creative and engaging marketing campaigns, which often feature playful and humorous themes. Whether you're a long-time fan or a first-time customer, the Free Cone Day event is a great opportunity to enjoy some delicious ice cream while also supporting a brand that cares about its customers and communities.

data.title: Middle-Aged Women: The Hidden Battle with Eating Disorders
data.description:
Eating disorders among middle-aged women are more prevalent than commonly believed, according to experts. Three women have shared their personal experiences, shedding light on the prevalence and impact of these conditions on individuals in this age group.

One eating disorder expert explained that societal pressure to maintain a thin figure can lead to a distorted sense of self-worth and value, particularly as women age. This idea that thinness equates to youth, vitality, and relevance can be particularly challenging for women experiencing physical aging.

Parents may not be fully aware of the complexities of eating disorders in children and teens, making it difficult for them to understand and support their loved ones. It is crucial for parents to educate themselves about the signs and symptoms of eating disorders to provide appropriate support and intervention.

Eating disorders are associated with a wide range of physical comorbidities, making them one of the most lethal psychiatric disorders. These conditions can have serious health consequences, including organ damage, bone loss, and even death. Early intervention and treatment are essential to prevent long-term complications.

One woman, after suffering from a lifelong eating disorder that nearly killed her, found relief by adopting a carnivore diet. However, experts caution that while this diet may have worked for her, it may not be suitable for everyone and should be approached with caution. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before making significant changes to one's diet.

Health professionals urge individuals to come forward and seek support for eating disorders. Breaking the stigma surrounding these conditions is crucial to encourage more people to seek help and access the treatment they need.

Compassion has been identified as a key transdiagnostic target that can buffer and reduce clinical symptoms of various mental health conditions, including eating disorders. Cultivating compassion for oneself and others can be an essential component of recovery and overall well-being.

One woman, at the age of 61, transformed her body by making two simple yet powerful changes to her diet and exercise. Her story highlights the importance of maintaining a healthy lifestyle, regardless of age, and the potential benefits of making sustainable changes to one's habits.

In conclusion, eating disorders among middle-aged women are a pressing issue that requires greater awareness and understanding. By sharing personal stories, educating parents, and promoting compassion, we can work towards breaking the stigma and supporting individuals affected by these conditions.

data.title: Melinda French Gates: IBM's Loss, Microsoft's Gain
data.description:
Melinda French Gates, the co-founder of the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation, had an unconventional start to her career in the tech industry. In a recent interview, she revealed that she initially received a job offer from ibm, but a hiring manager at the company advised her to turn it down. The reason? They believed that microsoft, a then-small startup, would be a better fit for her.

This advice, which left French Gates "dumbfounded," proved to be a turning point in her career. She joined Microsoft in 1987, where she worked as a project manager for the company's operating systems. Her time at Microsoft was instrumental in shaping her career and eventually led to her co-founding the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation with her husband, Bill Gates.

French Gates is not the only prominent figure to have received career-changing advice from unexpected sources. Jensen Huang, the CEO of nvidia, was advised by his father to pursue a career in engineering instead of his initial plan to study medicine. Indra Nooyi, the former CEO of PepsiCo, was encouraged by her father to pursue a career in business after she initially wanted to become a classical musician. Steve Jobs, the co-founder of Apple, was advised by his mentor, Mike Markkula, to drop out of college and focus on his passion for computers.

These stories highlight the importance of mentorship and the value of seeking advice from unexpected sources. They also underscore the unpredictable nature of career paths and the potential for unexpected opportunities to arise. As French Gates' experience demonstrates, sometimes the best career moves come from the most unexpected places.

data.title: Southland Holdings Q4 2024: Key Contradictions in Backlog, Cash Flow, and Tax Rate Trends
data.description:
These are the key contradictions discussed in Southland Holdings' latest 2024Q4 earnings call, specifically including: Non-M&P Legacy Backlog Completion Timeline, Cash Flow from Operations Expectations, Backlog and Revenue Growth Expectations, and Tax Rate Trends:



Backlog and Project Pipeline:
- Southland Holdings ended the quarter with $2.57 billion of backlog, including $2.3 billion from new core projects, and has $750 million in pending alternative delivery contracts.
- This was driven by a focus on completing legacy work, winding down the M&P business, and prioritizing high-quality new core projects.

Financial Performance and Challenges:
- The company reported $267 million in Q4 revenue and a gross profit of $8 million, with unfavorable adjustments impacting results by $27 million.
- The challenges were attributed to dispute resolutions and increased completion costs on legacy projects.

Tax and Effective Tax Rate:
- Southland reported an income tax benefit of $14 million for the quarter and an effective tax rate of 31% for the year, despite a pre-tax loss.
- This was due to changes in effective tax rate, deferred tax liabilities, and adjustments to forecasted year-end results.

Safety and Operational Excellence:
- Multiple projects, such as Ashbridges Bay Outfall Tunnel and SELA 26, surpassed 1 million safe work hours without a lost time incident.
- This achievement highlights the company's strong safety culture and commitment to operational excellence.

Infrastructure Investment and Bidding Outlook:
- Southland anticipates a similar pattern of new project awards ramping up in the back half of the year, with a focus on high-quality projects in core markets.
- This strategy is aimed at maintaining profitability while benefiting from a favorable infrastructure investment environment.

data.title: Dow Jones Gains Fade Amid Tariff Talk; Fed Report Next; Nvidia Wavers As Rivals' Earnings Loom
data.description:
The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) experienced a brief respite from its recent slump on Tuesday, but gains quickly faded as investors grappled with the potential impact of President Trump's tariff announcements. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite also took a hit, with the latter entering correction territory. The 10-year Treasury yield fell to its lowest level since October, reflecting investor concerns about the economy's ability to withstand incoming tariffs.



The upcoming Federal Reserve report is expected to have a significant impact on market reactions and investment decisions. Traders will be closely watching the announced interest rate level and the content of the report to gauge the Fed's views on the economy and its policy propensity. The market surprise component and sentiment score are important predictors of the financial markets' reaction, and applying non-linear models could result in better prediction of market reaction. However, external factors, such as global pandemics, can also influence market reactions to the Fed's report.

Nvidia's stock price and market position could be affected by the earnings reports of its competitors. AMD's strong earnings reports and market share gains in the CPU and GPU markets have been seen as a threat to Nvidia's dominance. Intel's struggles in the CPU market could also open up opportunities for nvidia to gain market share in the GPU market. Nvidia's competitors' earnings reports can provide insights into market demand, technological advancements, investor sentiment, supply chain dynamics, and pricing strategies, all of which can impact Nvidia's stock price and market position.



In conclusion, the recent tariff announcements have had a significant impact on the djia and other major indices in the short term, but the long-term impact is less certain. The upcoming Federal Reserve report is expected to have a significant impact on market reactions and investment decisions, and Nvidia's stock price and market position could be affected by the earnings reports of its competitors. Traders and investors should stay informed and adapt their strategies accordingly to navigate the ever-changing market landscape.

data.title: ADP Employment Change: Goods out produces services for the first time since 2018
data.description:
The latest ADP Employment Change report for February 2025 revealed that private-sector employment increased by 77,000 jobs, a significant slowdown from January’s 183,000 gain and well below the expected 140,000. This marks the smallest job gain since July 2024, signaling a potential deceleration in hiring momentum as businesses navigate policy uncertainty and weakening consumer spending. ADP's Chief Economist, Nela Richardson, noted that employers appear hesitant to hire, reflecting broader concerns about economic conditions.

Sector Breakdown: Goods vs. Services Diverge

Hiring trends diverged sharply across industries, with goods-producing sectors adding 42,000 jobs, while services-producing sectors saw a muted 36,000.

- Goods-Producing Sectors:

- Construction: +26,000

- Manufacturing: +18,000

- Natural Resources/Mining: -2,000

- Service-Providing Sectors:

- Leisure & Hospitality: +41,000 (Strongest service sector growth)

- Professional & Business Services: +27,000

- Financial Activities: +26,000

- Trade, Transportation & Utilities: -33,000 (Largest sector loss)

- Education & Health Services: -28,000

- Information: -14,000

The goods-producing sector continued to show resilience, with construction and manufacturing leading job gains, likely supported by ongoing infrastructure projects and corporate investment in industrial production. On the other hand, trade, transportation, and education sectors lost jobs, reflecting softer demand and possible cost-cutting measures by employers.

Wage Growth Holds Steady, But Job Changers See Slower Gains

Pay growth remains a critical focus for Federal Reserve policymakers as they assess inflationary pressures. According to ADP’s Pay Insights report, annual pay growth for job-stayers held steady at 4.7 percent, while job-changers saw a slight slowdown to 6.7 percent from 6.8 percent in January.

- Goods Sector Pay Gains:

- Construction: 4.9 percent

- Manufacturing: 4.8 percent

- Natural Resources/Mining: 4.4 percent

- Service Sector Pay Gains:

- Financial Activities: 5.1 percent

- Education & Health Services: 4.9 percent

- Trade, Transportation & Utilities: 4.4 percent

- Information: 4.2 percent

Notably, smaller businesses continue to struggle with wage growth, with firms having 1-19 employees seeing just a 2.8 percent increase in annual pay, compared to 5.0 percent at large firms (500+ employees). This suggests larger corporations may have more pricing power and financial flexibility to retain talent in a competitive job market.

Labor Market Momentum and Market Implications

This ADP report comes at a time of increased scrutiny over the health of the U.S. labor market. The weaker-than-expected job growth raises concerns about whether the economy is entering a slowdown, especially given the recent fluctuations in ADP data:

- January 2025: +183K (above expectations)

- December 2024: +122K (missed expectations)

- October 2024: +233K (significant beat)

The volatile nature of ADP prints in recent months suggests that hiring demand is cooling, which could influence the Federal Reserve’s next policy decisions. While markets expect 50 basis points of rate cuts this year, this report may increase pressure on the Fed to ease monetary policy sooner if labor weakness persists.

Fed & Market Reaction: Will This Impact Rate Cut Expectations?

The Federal Reserve has remained cautious about its rate-cut timeline, but a weakening labor market could force a reconsideration of its stance. The February report reinforces hiring hesitancy among businesses and raises questions about whether higher interest rates are weighing on labor demand.

Market participants will also be watching Friday’s Nonfarm Payrolls report, which often provides a broader and more detailed look at employment trends. If NFP shows a similar slowdown, it could strengthen the case for earlier Fed rate cuts and weigh on the U.S. Dollar Index (DXY), which is already trading near key support levels.

Final Thoughts: A Warning Sign or Just a Soft Patch?

The February ADP report adds to the growing debate over the U.S. labor market's resilience. While some industries—particularly construction, financial activities, and professional services—continue to see strong hiring, the broader slowdown in job creation suggests businesses are pulling back amid economic uncertainty.

With wage growth holding steady but slowing for job changers, inflation concerns may take a backseat to employment risks in upcoming Federal Reserve discussions. Investors will now shift their focus to the Nonfarm Payrolls report and any signals from the Fed on potential shifts in monetary policy.

data.title: Arbe Robotics Q4 2024: Navigating Contradictions in Radar Adoption, Production Timelines, and Competitive Edge
data.description:
These are the key contradictions discussed in Arbe Robotics Ltd.'s latest 2024Q4 earnings call, specifically including: Imaging Radar Adoption and Market Demand, Production Timeline and Revenue Ramp, Production and Revenue Timeline, and Competitive Positioning:



Revenue and Financial Performance:
- Arbe Robotics reported total revenue of $0.1 million for Q4 2024, compared to $0.35 million in Q4 2023, with full-year revenue of $0.8 million in 2024, down from $1.5 million in 2023.
- The decline in revenue was primarily due to delayed decision-making timelines by automakers and lower-than-expected product shipments.

Fundraising and Financial Position:
- The company successfully raised nearly $17 million through various fundraising initiatives, including an underwritten registered direct offering and the conversion of convertible bonds.
- As of December 31, 2024, Arbe had $24.6 million in cash, with an additional $54.5 million raised in January 2025, providing a robust balance sheet.

2025 Revenue and EBITDA Guidance:
- Arbe expects annual revenue in the range of $2 million to $5 million for 2025, with a focus on the latter part of the year.
- Adjusted EBITDA for 2025 is projected to be in the range of $29 million loss to $35 million loss.

Product and Partnership Developments:
- Arbe announced collaborations with key players such as NVIDIA and HiRain technologies, and significant contracts like the one with Tianyi Transportation Technology worth an estimated $7.3 million.
- These developments are crucial for market penetration and expanding the company's addressable market, supporting the goal of shipping around 5 million imaging radar chipsets by 2030.

Operating Expenses and Cash Position:
- Total operating expenses for the full year of 2024 were $48.9 million, a slight increase from $46.8 million in 2023, primarily due to non-cash share-based compensation expenses.
- Post-fundraising, Arbe's cash position is above $70 million, with a share count of 85.7 million.

data.title: Teleperformance: Navigating Shareholder Dynamics and Voting Rights
data.description:
Teleperformance se, a global leader in digital business services, recently disclosed its monthly information regarding shares and voting rights. This update provides valuable insights into the company's capital structure and shareholder dynamics, which can significantly impact investor decisions and market trends. As of October 31, 2024, Teleperformance SE reported a total number of shares composing the share capital of the company at 60,738,823. Additionally, the company revealed a total number of gross voting rights at 62,230,653 and a total number of net voting rights at 60,816,193, after deducting shares deprived of voting rights. The company's bylaws do not require disclosure of crossing thresholds beyond the mandatory levels set by law, indicating a lack of additional transparency provisions.

These changes in voting rights and share capital may have implications for investors, both in terms of marketability and liquidity. The reduction in shares deprived of voting rights suggests a decrease in the number of treasury shares held by the company, which could potentially enhance market capitalization if the stock price remains constant or increases. However, the absence of a provision requiring additional threshold disclosures might impact investor sentiment and decision-making, as it could be perceived as a lack of transparency, potentially hindering liquidity in the long term.

To maintain a balanced and analytical perspective, investors should consider multiple factors when evaluating these changes, including earnings reports, market sentiment, and geopolitical dynamics. While the absence of additional disclosure provisions may impact investor sentiment, it is essential to weigh this against other factors that could influence the company's stock price and trading volume. By staying informed and adaptable, investors can make well-informed decisions and capitalize on opportunities in the global markets.



In conclusion, Teleperformance SE's recent disclosure of share capital and voting rights provides valuable insights into the company's capital structure and shareholder dynamics. While these changes may have implications for marketability and liquidity, investors should consider multiple factors when evaluating these developments. By staying informed and adaptable, investors can make well-informed decisions and capitalize on opportunities in the global markets.

data.title: BlockDAG's $200M Presale Surge Amidst Solana, Ethereum, and Dogecoin Gains
data.description:
BlockDAG's $200.7M Presale Garners Attention Amidst Gains by Solana, Ethereum, and Dogecoin

In the dynamic world of cryptocurrencies, several projects have been making waves, each with its unique offerings. While Solana, Ethereum, and Dogecoin continue to dominate the market, a new player, BlockDAG, is rapidly gaining traction with its record-breaking presale.

BlockDAG's presale has raised nearly $200.7 million, with over 18.7 billion bdag coins sold. This remarkable achievement comes as the project prepares for listings on 10 major centralized exchanges (CEXs). The price of BDAG has surged by 2,380%, from $0.001 to $0.0248, delivering substantial returns ahead of its CEX listings.

What sets BlockDAG apart is its innovative architecture. Unlike traditional blockchains, BlockDAG employs a parallel transaction processing system, eliminating bottlenecks that hinder scalability in older networks. This design significantly enhances transaction throughput while maintaining security and decentralization, making it an attractive choice for DeFi applications and smart contracts.

Meanwhile, established projects like Solana, Ethereum, and Dogecoin continue to grow and adapt. Solana's fast and cost-effective transactions continue to attract users and developers, with increasing involvement in DeFi, nfts, and Web3 projects. Ethereum's transition to Proof-of-Stake is poised to enhance its efficiency and scalability, reinforcing its position as a dominant blockchain for DeFi and NFT applications. Dogecoin, once a meme coin, has transformed into a widely accepted digital currency, driven by community enthusiasm and growing real-world applications.

As competition among blockchain networks intensifies, BlockDAG is capturing the market's attention. Its upcoming exchange listings are expected to boost trading activity and accessibility, positioning it as a rising force in the industry. With a successful presale, a user base of over 170,000, and a significant price increase, BlockDAG is establishing itself as one of the best crypto projects to watch now.

data.title: "Bitcoin's Volatility: Retail Investors Accumulate Amidst Market Uncertainty"
data.description:
Bitcoin's recent price volatility has sparked a surge in retail accumulation, as investors seek to capitalize on market uncertainties and potential recovery scenarios. The significant decline in Bitcoin prices, combined with a surge in buying from retail investors, indicates a strategic shift in market sentiment.

According to Glassnode, the recent market sell-offs have led to a redistribution of Bitcoin, with roughly one-third of the BTC accumulated at $96k-$97.5k in February 2025 being redistributed. This redistribution suggests that weaker hands aimed to mitigate losses amidst plummeting prices, underscoring a behavioral shift among investors. However, some investors from the $96K price range have started accumulating Bitcoin around $83K, forming a distinctive stair-step pattern.

The buying activity is predominantly observed among long-term investors, particularly at significant price levels of $84.2K (23K BTC) and $86.9K (25.8K BTC). When investors opt to buy during downturns, it creates organic demand that can potentially boost Bitcoin’s price and set the stage for recoveries. Indeed, recent trends show prices have temporarily rebounded, peaking at around $87K.

Amidst an environment where weak hands were quick to sell, other savvy investors took advantage of the price drop to accumulate Bitcoin. This behavior is reflected in data showing a re-entry of retail traders, even as market uncertainty persists. Importantly, the new issuance remains robust at over 13K BTC, with crabs (1-10 BTC) increasing to 15K and fish (10-100 BTC) at 5.5K. This indicates a resilient presence of retail traders at current price levels, reflecting growing market confidence and demand.

COINOTAG has noted that the negative Bitcoin exchange netflows throughout the past week suggest an ongoing accumulation strategy by investors. The growing outflows indicate heightened market confidence as traders view current prices as a valuable purchasing opportunity. As retail holders increasingly engage in buying during this period of decline, large entities have maintained a more passive stance regarding accumulation. This trend is crucial since it indicates a scarcity of Bitcoin in the market, reinforcing potential recovery dynamics where a low supply combined with high demand drives prices higher.

Retail participants have displayed a willingness to purchase amidst market declines, yet their speculative nature reacts sensitively to news and broader economic

data.title: Trump's Bitcoin Reserve: A Game Changer for Crypto
data.description:
The Trump administration has hinted at a strategic Bitcoin reserve, sparking speculation about the potential implications for the cryptocurrency industry. U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick has suggested that President Trump will unveil a Bitcoin-only reserve at the upcoming White House Crypto Summit on March 7.

In an interview, Lutnick clarified that Bitcoin will likely have a special status in the country’s national cryptocurrency reserve, which will also include Ether, Solana, Cardano, and XRP. He stated, "The president definitely thinks that there’s a Bitcoin strategic reserve. Now, there will be the question of, how do we handle the other cryptocurrencies. And I think the model is going to be announced on Friday when we do that."

President Trump has faced criticism for including assets other than Bitcoin in the country’s crypto reserve. Critics argue that centralized altcoins should not be included alongside Bitcoin, which is the only decentralized commodity that can be used as a long-term store of wealth. Even notorious Bitcoin critic Peter Schiff, who refused to invest in the digital asset when it was valued at less than $100, said he understands the rationale behind a BTC-only reserve but not one that includes altcoins.

In response, Lutnick reiterated Trump’s interest in a Bitcoin-only stockpile without dismissing the other assets in the proposed basket. He said, "So Bitcoin is one thing, and then the other currencies, the other crypto tokens, I think, will be treated differently — positively, but differently."

The White House Crypto Summit, scheduled for March 7, is expected to provide more clarity on the Trump administration's plans for the cryptocurrency industry. The invite list includes prominent figures such as Strategy founder Michael Saylor, chainlink co-founder Sergey Nazarov, and coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong. The summit reflects the U.S. government’s historic pivot toward digital assets under President Trump, who vowed to make America the blockchain and crypto capital of the world during the election.

The summit comes as anticipation builds over the Trump administration's stance on a potential U.S. cryptocurrency reserve. Lutnick has hinted that investors should expect more details about the potential Bitcoin reserve at the event. The market appears to be reacting positively to the potential of a U.S. Bitcoin reserve, with some reports indicating a 6% increase in the price of Bitcoin.

data.title: Full Truck Alliance's Q4 2024: Navigating Contradictions in Fulfillment Rates, User Strategies, and Order Growth
data.description:
These are the key contradictions discussed in Full Truck Alliance Co. Ltd.'s latest 2024Q4 earnings call, specifically including: Fulfillment Rate Improvement, User Acquisition and Engagement Strategies, and Order Volume Growth Expectations:



Order Volume Growth and Market Leadership:
- Full Truck Alliance reported a significant 24% year-over-year increase in fulfilled orders in Q4 2024.
- The growth was driven by robust network effects, efficient user acquisition strategies, and enhanced transaction efficiency.

Fulfillment Rate Improvement:
- The company's fulfillment rate reached 37.5% in Q4, up 5.4% year-over-year.
- Improvements were due to increases in low and medium frequency shipper contributions, optimized order matching, and refined operational strategies.

Transaction Service Revenue Growth:
- Transaction service revenues surged over 70% year-over-year to RMB1.16 billion, contributing 36% of total net revenue.
- The growth was primarily driven by increased order volume, refined monetization strategies, and improved monetized order penetration ratio.

Dividend and Share Repurchase Initiatives:
- The company initiated a semiannual dividend policy, with an initial payout of $200 million for 2025, and a new share repurchase program of up to $200 million by March 2026.
- These initiatives reflect the company's strong cash position and confidence in business growth, aimed at delivering value to shareholders.

data.title: WTF? Trump Charges $5 Million for Dinner Meetings
data.description:
Business leaders are reportedly paying as much as $5 million for a one-on-one meeting with President Trump at his Mar-a-Lago estate, while others are spending $1 million each to attend a group dinner, according to sources who spoke with WIRED.

At a recent candlelight dinner this past Saturday, guests were invited to pay $1 million to secure a seat, according to an invitation obtained by WIRED. The invitation, sent under the "MAGA Inc." banner, read:"You are invited to a candlelight dinner featuring special guest President Donald J. Trump."

MAGA Inc., short for Make America Great Again Inc., is associated with Trump's political operations.

Trump's Private Meetings: A Hot Ticket for Business Leaders

The invitation clarifies that Trump is attending solely as a guest speaker and is not soliciting direct donations. However, sources describe the $5 million private meetings as a highly sought-after opportunity among business elites.

What remains unclear is where the money is going and how it will be used. A source familiar with the event claimed that "all funds will go toward the library"—a reference to Trump's proposed presidential library, which he has yet to build after leaving office.

Experts Raise Concerns Over Ethics and Influence

While sitting presidents in their second term have historically held fundraising events for their political parties, experts say the timing, venue, and high price tag of Trump's Mar-a-Lago gatherings are unprecedented.

"I can't recall a sitting president, within weeks of taking office, soliciting millions in fundraising," said Don Moynihan, a professor of public policy at the University of Michigan."The concern is less about fundraising and more about access and influence ... People hoping for favorable treatment see it in their interest to donate to Trump."

Moynihan also highlighted the lack of ethical safeguards in the Trump administration, where the boundaries between Trump's business empire and the presidency appear increasingly blurred.

data.title: U.S. Delays Auto Tariffs on Canada, Mexico Amidst Trade Talks
data.description:
The U.S. administration is reportedly considering a one-month delay in implementing auto tariffs on Canada and Mexico, according to sources familiar with the matter. This potential move comes as the U.S. has been engaged in ongoing trade negotiations with both countries, aiming to reach a mutually beneficial agreement.

Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick hinted that President Trump is already considering tariff concessions, suggesting that the U.S. may be open to negotiations. However, Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau has stated that he is not willing to lift Canada's retaliatory tariffs if Trump leaves any tariffs on Canada. Mexico, on the other hand, has indicated that it will respond to the U.S. tariffs with retaliatory measures of its own.

The U.S. officially imposed new 25% tariffs on Canada and Mexico on March 4, 2025, following through on a long-delayed pledge from President Donald Trump. The tariffs, which were initially announced in 2018, have been a source of contention between the U.S. and its North American neighbors. The U.S. has argued that the tariffs are necessary to protect American jobs and industries, while Canada and Mexico have maintained that the tariffs are unfair and will harm economic growth.

The implementation of the tariffs has set off a day of anger, retaliation, and market unease. Global markets fell after the steep U.S. tariffs on China, Canada, and Mexico went into effect. Businesses have been scrambling to contain the fallout from the tariffs, which have disrupted supply chains and increased costs for consumers and producers alike.

In response to the tariffs, Wall Street's losing streak deepened, with stocks falling sharply in the aftermath of the announcement. However, Commerce Secretary Lutnick suggested that the U.S. may be open to a compromise, stating that the U.S. would likely meet Canada and Mexico "in the middle" on tariffs. This potential compromise could help to ease tensions between the U.S. and its North American neighbors and mitigate the economic impact of the tariffs.

data.title: Crescent Energy's Production Plunge: A 5% Drop!
Riskified's Q4 2024: Key Contradictions in Strategy, Competition, and Growth Expectations
data.description:
These are the key contradictions discussed in Riskified's latest 2024Q4 earnings call, specifically including: Strategic Initiatives, Competitive Landscape, Product Offerings, Net Dollar Retention Rate Expectations, and Platform Sales Strategy:



Revenue and Growth in 2024:
- Riskified reported revenue of $327.5 million for 2024, exceeding the high end of their guidance, with a 10% year-over-year increase.
- Growth was driven by an increase in GMV by 15%, strong performance in new business activity, and an improvement in adjusted EBITDA margins in their first full year of positive adjusted EBITDA.

Customer Retention and Renewals:
- The company achieved an ADR (annual dollar retention rate) and NDR (net dollar retention rate) that were below historical benchmarks.
- This was due to factors such as churn events in certain verticals and increased competitive pressure, but efforts are underway to improve retention rates in 2025 through multiyear contract commitments.

Expansion into New Verticals:
- Riskified derived $45 million in revenue from new merchants added in 2024, with notable wins in verticals like money transfer and payments, and food.
- This expansion was driven by a strategic focus on penetrating newer verticals and landing key accounts outside of the Americas and EMEA.

Product Platform Growth:
- New product revenue increased by 90% year-over-year, with these products accounting for 10% of total bookings won in 2024.
- The growth in the multi-product platform is attributed to increased adoption and the value it provides through diverse use cases beyond the core chargeback guarantee.

Operational and Cost Management:
- Operating expenses declined by 4% in 2024, with a focus on efficiency and leveraging AI tools to automate tasks and reduce manual work.
- This was part of a broader strategy to optimize operational levers and drive toward profitability, aiming for adjusted EBITDA margins between 15% to 20% by the end of 2026.

data.title: Warning! Options Traders Are Betting on a U.S Stock Crash
data.description:
Professional options traders are hedging against a market crash as demand for deep out-of-the-money VIX calls surges.

Mandy Xu of cboe global markets shared with MarketWatch on Monday that demand for deep out-of-the-money (OTM) call options tied to the Cboe Volatility Index (VIX), surged last week.

The options market is dominated by professional traders, who are betting on a potential sharp spike in VIX. These investors are willing to take relatively low-cost positions in exchange for potentially high returns, a typical hedging strategy against a market crash.

The term deep out-of-the-money (Deep OTM) refers to options where the strike price is significantly distant from the current price of the underlying asset. Data indicates that demand for deeply OTM VIX call options has surged—these options will only become profitable if VIX rises above 50.

According to a Cboe report, last Thursday saw purchases of over 260,000 VIX call options expiring in May with strike prices between 55 and 75, amounting to a total cost of $10.7 million. A Cboe representative noted that this was the second-highest volume day ever recorded for high-strike (50+) VIX call options. Net customer purchases of deeply OTM VIX calls soared to nearly 250,000 contracts, marking the largest one-day transaction volume since May 4, 2023.

FactSet data shows that VIX has not closed above 50 since March 2020. Last Friday, the index settled just below 20, a level consistent with its long-term average. The 20-point threshold is crucial: a VIX reading above 20 indicates heightened market turbulence, while a level below 20 suggests relative market stability.

data.title: Bonduelle's Strong First Half: Branded Activities Drive Growth and Profitability
data.description:
The Bonduelle Group, a French S.C.A (Partnership limited by Shares) with a capital of 57 102 699,50 euros, registered under number 447 250 044 (Dunkerque Commercial and Companies Register), has posted impressive financial results for the first half of the 2024-2025 fiscal year. The company's current operating income grew by a strong +17.8% on a like-for-like basis, amounting to 48 million euros at current exchange rates and 47.8 million euros at constant exchange rates compared with 40.6 million euros the previous fiscal year. This growth is a testament to the company's strategic focus on branded activities and operational excellence.

Over the period from July 1 to December 31, 2024, the Bonduelle Group's sales for the 1st half year of fiscal year 2024-2025 amounted to 1,119.4 million euros, compared with 1,139.2 million euros for the 1st half of the previous fiscal year. This represents a slight downturn of -1.5% on a like-for-like basis and -1.7% on reported figures. Despite this slight downturn, the company's branded activities grew by a clear +1.9% over the period, contrasting with the marked decline in the private label segment, which experienced a -6.9% decrease.

The growth in branded activities is a key strategic focus for the company, as announced in their "Transform to win" transformation plan. This plan is based on solid fundamentals, including a modern corporate mission that puts positive impact at the heart of the business activity, flagship brands, and a strong connection with the agricultural sector. The plan aims to prepare for the rebound and acceleration of the group's performance in the coming years.

As part of this transformation plan, the Bonduelle Group has implemented several strategic initiatives that have contributed to its financial results. These initiatives include brand development, operational excellence, reorganization of the business portfolio, return to growth in the United States, and the deployment of a sustainable business model. These initiatives have helped the group achieve its strong growth in current operating income and branded activities, despite a slight downturn in business activity over the 1st half year and an expected negative impact on profitability in the 2nd half year due to the poor agricultural harvest in Russia.

The company's Chief Executive Officer, Xavier Unkovic, has stated that the 2024-2025 1st half year is already posting clear results over the five strategic pillars which represent the solid foundations of the group's transformation between now and 2027. The roadmap is clear, and the company's teams are fully committed to reaching their objectives. The company is gearing up for a spring full of activations, communication on its brands and innovations, which are key factors in its transformation.

In conclusion, the Bonduelle Group's strong financial results for the first half of the 2024-2025 fiscal year are a testament to the company's strategic focus on branded activities and operational excellence. Despite a slight downturn in business activity, the company's branded activities grew by a clear +1.9%, contributing to the group's overall financial performance. The company's "Transform to win" transformation plan, based on solid fundamentals, has helped the group achieve its strong growth in current operating income and branded activities. The company is well-positioned to continue its transformation and achieve its long-term objectives.

data.title: Dairy Workers' Victory: Higher Wages, Better Benefits, and Stronger Protections
data.description:
In a significant victory for dairy workers, Teamsters Local 120 members at Foremost Farms USA have overwhelmingly ratified a strong four-year agreement. This new contract promises higher wages, increased pension contributions, and improved health care for the workers. Brad Lusk, a Local 120 shop steward, expressed his satisfaction with the outcome, stating, "This company has generated a lot of revenue because of our hard work. We fought hard for this contract, and now every worker at this company is better off because we were ready to strike to get what we deserved."



The deal comes after workers forced management to take their demands seriously by resoundingly authorizing a strike. In addition to better pay and benefits, Lusk and his co-workers also secured a tripled premium rate for night shifts, more bereavement time, and double the allowance for work boots. Tiffany Kagel, a business agent for Local 120, emphasized the importance of recognizing workers' contributions, saying, "It’s about time Foremost Farms USA starts to recognize what Teamsters members have known all along. Workers are responsible for the success of this company, so it’s only right that we get great health care and other benefits."



Cory Holliday, a shop steward for Local 120, echoed the sentiment, stating, "We’re willing to do whatever it takes to get a fair contract. So is every other Teamster at this company." Local 120 and Teamsters Local 662 represent workers at nearly all of Foremost Farms USA’s facilities, with Teamsters Local 120 proudly representing over 15,000 workers in Wisconsin, Minnesota, Iowa, North Dakota, and South Dakota.



This contract ratification highlights the power of collective bargaining and the importance of recognizing and rewarding workers' contributions. As the dairy industry faces labor shortages and an aging workforce, attracting and retaining skilled workers becomes increasingly crucial. By offering competitive wages, improved benefits, and better working conditions, Foremost Farms USA and other dairy companies can address these challenges and ensure the long-term sustainability of the industry.



Moreover, this victory sets a precedent for other dairy companies, encouraging them to offer similar benefits and compensation packages to attract and retain skilled workers. This could lead to a more competitive labor market within the dairy industry, with companies vying for the best talent. As the dairy industry continues to evolve and face new challenges, it is essential to prioritize the well-being and satisfaction of its workforce to ensure its long-term success.



In conclusion, the ratification of this new contract by Teamsters Local 120 members at Foremost Farms USA is a significant milestone in the dairy industry's ongoing efforts to address labor shortages and attract and retain skilled workers. By recognizing and rewarding workers' contributions, the industry can ensure its long-term sustainability and competitiveness in the global market.
